texto: In an age when mass pleasures like television are becoming more feeble and homogeneous, the very act of discrimination becomes a form of protest. At a time when mass marketing of food produces a product so disgusting that it has to be wrapped in distracting gimmicks to be sold, the mere fact of paying attention to what you eat and drink and telling the truth about taste is a revolutionary act.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Lynn Hoffman: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de passagem do tempo e prioridades. O trecho “In an age when mass pleasures like television are becoming more feeble and homogeneous, the very …” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que reflexao, beer, food pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de food e knowledge.
